# SECURITY_AUDIT — GLM Safety & Compatibility Review

*Generated by glm-5.1*

---

## Overall Verdict: ✅ SAFE TO IMPLEMENT

The security audit overlaps ~80% with the bug report. The same risk analysis applies. Every fix addresses an actual vulnerability. The "breaking" changes are intentional security hardening.

---

## ✅ Safe to fix immediately

| Item | Why Safe |
|------|----------|
| S-01 (extension.pem removal) | Private key already exposed — removing it is purely protective |
| S-02 (backend.env credentials) | Credentials already hardcoded — rotating is purely protective |
| S-05 (postMessage '*') | Data already leaking — restricting origin is purely protective |
| S-06 (key create/revoke auth) | Any key can already create keys — adding master key guard is purely protective |
| S-08 (pkill -9 restart) | Restart already broken — fixing only restores functionality |
| S-10 (storage.local.clear) | Data already destroyed on reset — adding confirmation only helps |
| S-15 (destructive message validation) | Messages already unvalidated — adding checks is purely protective |
| S-16 (asyncio.run in payments) | Notifications already broken — fixing only restores them |
| S-17 (QR endpoint auth) | Endpoint already unauthenticated — adding guard is purely protective |
| S-18 (login logging) | Credentials already logged — removing them is purely protective |
| S-19 (JSON injection) | Audit log already vulnerable — fixing is purely protective |
| S-20/S-21 (backup atomicity) | Backups already corrupt — fixing only helps |
| S-22 (ONNX blocking) | Event loop already blocked — offloading only helps |
| S-23 (cache thread safety) | Crash already guaranteed under load — adding locks only helps |
| S-24 (rate limiter) | Limiter already permanently broken — fixing only restores it |

---

## ⚠️ Moderate risk — safe IF done carefully

| Item | Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------|------------|
| **S-01** (extension.pem) | Must generate new signing key; existing extension updates may fail if store expects old key | Generate new key, update CI signing, bump extension version |
| **S-02** (backend.env credentials) | Must rotate credentials; update all deployment configs simultaneously | Rotate in one coordinated step |
| **S-03** (HTTP default API URL) | Existing deployments using HTTP will need to update URL | Change default but **allow HTTP override** with a warning |
| **S-04** (new Function(code)) | Restricting code execution could break existing userscripts that rely on dynamic execution | Validate sender origin — let signed/known scripts continue to execute |
| **S-07** (API key plaintext storage) | Encrypting existing keys requires migration | Add encryption wrapper, migrate on next extension load |
| **S-09** (sender validation) | Adding origin checks could reject legitimate messages from content scripts | Whitelist `chrome-extension://` origin + target site origins |
| **S-11** (alert/confirm override scope) | Overrides will no longer apply on non-target sites | Intentional. Verify no workflow depends on overrides on other sites |
| **S-12** (fetch/XHR override scope) | Interceptors will no longer apply on non-target sites | Intentional. Same as S-11 |
| **S-13** (getUserMedia override) | Adding consent prompts changes existing flow | Users must opt-in; document the new step |
| **S-14** (browsingData scope) | **Nuclear restart and browser restart will no longer wipe ALL data** | Intentional. But some workflows may depend on full wipe — verify with user |
| **S-25** (relative paths) | Changing to absolute paths could break existing deployments | Use `Path(__file__).parent` resolution; add override via env var |
| **S-26** (weak session cookie) | **All existing admin sessions invalidated** on next startup | One-time re-login required. Low impact. |
| **S-27/S-28** (path traversal fixes) | Path validation could reject previously-accessible paths | Only reject paths outside allowed directories — legitimate paths still work |

---

## 🟢 Low risk — long term improvements

| Item | Assessment |
|------|------------|
| S-29 | Direct key access — purely fixes a crash |
| S-30 | SRI for @require — additive, doesn't break existing scripts |
| S-31 | Missing env field — purely documentation |
| S-32 | xargs parsing — purely fixes a bug |
| S-33 | Unused variables — purely cleanup |
| S-34 | all_frames reduction — intentional scope restriction |
| S-35 | <all_urls> reduction — intentional scope restriction |
| S-36 | .gitignore addition — purely protective |
| S-37 | stop.bat — intentional restriction |
| S-38 | .dockerignore addition — purely protective |

---

## Exposed Secrets — Coordinated Rotation Required

These must be rotated in a single coordinated step:

| Secret | Location | Rotation Action |
|--------|----------|-----------------|
| RSA Extension Signing Private Key | `extension.pem` | Generate new key, update CI, bump extension version |
| Admin Token `123456` | `config/backend.env` | Change to strong random token, update all deployment configs |
| Admin Password `123456` | `config/backend.env` | Change to strong password, update all deployment configs |
| Docker Compose defaults | `docker-compose.yml` | Replace `test12345678` and `admin123` with env var references |

**WARNING:** Do NOT remove old credentials from git history until new credentials are deployed everywhere. Use `git filter-branch` or `bfg` AFTER rotation is complete.

---

## Critical Data Flows That Will Change

| Current (Insecure) | After Fix (Secure) | Impact |
|--------------------|---------------------|--------|
| `postMessage(data, '*')` | `postMessage(data, window.location.origin)` | Cross-origin frames can no longer read messages |
| `http://localhost:8780` | `https://...` (default) | API keys sent over encrypted channel |
| `chrome.storage.local` (plaintext) | Encrypted storage | API keys protected at rest |
| No sender validation | Origin validation on all messages | Untrusted content scripts can no longer trigger destructive actions |
| `<all_urls>` content scripts | Target-domain-only | Extension no longer injects into banking/email sites |

---

## What WILL break (intentionally)

- Any API key can no longer create/revoke other keys (S-06)
- QR image endpoint requires admin auth (S-17)
- `postMessage('*')` restricted to same-origin (S-05)
- `browsingData.remove({since:0})` scoped to specific origin (S-14)
- `alert`/`confirm` overrides scoped to target domain (S-11)
- Fetch/XHR interceptors scoped to target domain (S-12)
- `getUserMedia` requires user consent (S-13)
- Admin sessions invalidated after cookie fix (S-26)

**These are all intentional security hardening — they break insecure behavior, not working features.**
